sourcesContent:
import React from 'react'; import { Link, useParams } from 'react-router-dom'; import { Play, Clock, Target, BookOpen, Users, Shield, Scale } from 'lucide-react'; const ScenarioBriefing: React.FC = () => { const { id } = useParams<{ id: string }>(); const scenario = { _id: '1', title: 'The Inventory That Changed Everything', subtitle: 'First-Time Bail Application – Theft Offense', description: 'A sales executive is accused of theft after a valuable laptop goes missing during an inventory check. As his defense attorney, you must investigate the case, interview the client, research relevant laws, and argue for bail.', estimatedTime: 30, difficulty: 'Intermediate', practiceArea: 'Criminal Law', courtType: 'Magistrate Court', parties: [ { role: 'Defendant', name: 'Rajesh Kumar', description: 'Sales executive accused of theft. Claims innocence and proper procedure.' }, { role: 'Defense Attorney', name: 'You', description: 'Assigned to represent the defendant and uncover the truth.' }, { role: 'Prosecutor', name: 'State Prosecutor', description: 'Representing the state with circumstantial evidence against Rajesh.' }, { role: 'Key Witness', name: 'Prakash Mehta', description: 'Colleague who was present during the inventory check.' }, { role: 'Complainant', name: 'Mr. Desai', description: 'Store manager who reported the incident.' } ], learningObjectives: [ 'Conduct a structured client interview to gather critical facts', 'Research applicable legal provisions under IPC and CrPC', 'Draft and orally present a bail application', 'Respond to standard prosecution objections', 'Apply legal principles to real-world scenarios', 'Develop critical thinking and advocacy skills' ], skillFocus: [ 'Client Interviewing', 'Legal Research', 'Oral Advocacy', 'Case Preparation', 'Critical Thinking', 'Evidence Analysis' ] }; const getDifficultyIcon = (difficulty: string) => { switch (difficulty) { case 'Beginner': return '🌱'; case 'Intermediate': return '📊'; case 'Advanced': return '🔥'; case 'Expert': return '👑'; default: return '📚'; } }; return (
Back to All Scenarios
Legal Simulation

{scenario.title}

{scenario.subtitle}

{scenario.description}

{/* Main Content */}
{/* Mission Briefing */}

Mission Briefing

You are a junior defense attorney at a criminal law firm. Your supervising partner assigns you a new case: your client, Rajesh Kumar, has been arrested for alleged theft and is currently in police custody. A bail hearing is scheduled for today. You must prepare and argue his bail application.

The stakes are high. Rajesh is a 28-year-old sales executive at Vijay Electronics with no prior criminal record. He was charged with stealing a company laptop valued at ₹45,000 during an inventory check. The case seems straightforward, but something doesn't add up.

Your mission is to uncover the truth, build a compelling defense, and argue successfully for your client's bail. The courtroom awaits, and justice depends on your skills.

{/* Key Details */}

Scenario Details

Time Required
{scenario.estimatedTime} min
Court Type
{scenario.courtType}
{getDifficultyIcon(scenario.difficulty)}
Difficulty
{scenario.difficulty}

Important Note

This is a non-bailable offense under IPC 379. Your arguments will determine whether your client spends additional time in custody while the investigation continues or is granted bail pending trial.

{/* Key Parties */}

Key Parties & Roles

{scenario.parties.map((party, index) => (
{party.name}
{party.role}

{party.description}

))}
{/* Sidebar */}
{/* Learning Objectives */}

Learning Objectives

{scenario.learningObjectives.map((objective, index) => (
{index + 1}
{objective}
))}
{/* Skill Focus */}

Skill Focus Areas

{scenario.skillFocus.map((skill, index) => (
{skill}
))}
{/* Start Simulation */}

Ready to Begin?

Enter the virtual courtroom and put your legal skills to the test. Remember: Every detail matters in the pursuit of justice.

); }; export default ScenarioBriefing;
